title: U.S. Treasury Sanctions Notorious Virtual Currency Mixer Tornado Cash (initial OFAC action, August 8, 2022)
quote: In August 2022 OFAC sanctioned Tornado Cash, adding it to the SDN list, alleging it had been used to launder more than $7 billion in cryptocurrency, including funds stolen by North Korea's Lazarus Group. The November 2022 update added 53 Ethereum addresses associated with Tornado Cash smart contracts, including at least 20 immutable contracts.

title: Van Loon v. Department of the Treasury (5th Cir. No. 23-50669, November 26, 2024)
authors: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit
quote: The court held that the immutable smart contracts comprising Tornado Cash could not be classified as 'property' under IEEPA because they lacked the hallmarks of ownership, control, and exclusivity, ruling that OFAC exceeded its statutory authority by sanctioning them.

title: Treasury Department Delists Tornado Cash Following the Fifth Circuit's Decision
quote: On March 21, 2025, OFAC officially lifted sanctions on Tornado Cash, removing the associated addresses and effectively reversing one of the Biden administration's most controversial enforcement actions in the crypto space.

title: Launching Aztec — Ignition Chain and Alpha mainnet (Nov 2025)
quote: In November 2025 the Aztec Ignition Chain went live as the first decentralized L2 on Ethereum. With infrastructure in place and a unanimous community vote, the network entered Alpha — the first Layer 2 with a full execution environment for private smart contracts. All accounts, transactions, and execution itself can be completely private.

title: Aztec's Core Cryptography, Now in Noir (Barretenberg / CHONK)
quote: Aztec's cryptographic backend Barretenberg has evolved through multiple generations. The latest innovation, CHONK (Client-side Highly Optimized ploNK), is purpose-built for proving on phones and browsers and is what powers proof generation for the Alpha Network. Noir's proving-system-agnostic design supports Barretenberg, Halo2, Plonky2, or Groth16.

title: The New Architecture for Ethereum Privacy: Introducing RAILGUN v3
quote: RAILGUN is privacy middleware using zk-SNARKs to enable private DeFi transactions on Ethereum, BNB Chain, Polygon, and Arbitrum without sacrificing chain security. Because RAILGUN is just on-chain smart-contract logic, achieving privacy without a separate L2 validator set or a vulnerable bridge has two major advantages: security and decentralization. zk-SNARKs encrypt wallet balances, transaction history, and transaction details. For 0zk-to-0zk transactions, sender, recipient, token type, and amount remain totally private; only relayer and destination smart-contract addresses are publicly visible.

title: Blockchain Privacy and Regulatory Compliance: Towards a Practical Equilibrium
quote: Privacy Pools is a smart-contract-based privacy-enhancing protocol. Users publish a zero-knowledge proof demonstrating their funds (do not) originate from known (un-)lawful sources, without publicly revealing their entire transaction graph. This is achieved by proving membership in custom association sets that satisfy properties required by regulation or social consensus.

title: 0xbow unveils 'Privacy Pools,' a new blockchain privacy tool drawing from Vitalik Buterin's research
quote: Privacy Pools launched in March 2025 on Ethereum mainnet, processing $6 million in transaction volume across more than 1,500 users since launch. Vitalik Buterin made a deposit shortly after the launch on March 31. Privacy Pools is a keystone part of the Ethereum Foundation's Kohaku initiative.

title: Nocturne wind-down announcement (June 5, 2024)
quote: Hey all, some tough news. After shutting down the privacy protocol we built earlier last year, we've decided to wind down the company. The frontend will remain open for withdrawals until the end of this month.

title: Semaphore — a zero-knowledge protocol for anonymous interactions
quote: Semaphore is a generic, open-source privacy layer for Ethereum applications based on zk-SNARKs. It allows you to cast a message (e.g. a vote or endorsement) as a provable group member without revealing your identity, and provides a mechanism to prevent double-signaling.

title: Intro to Zero-Knowledge Proofs, Semaphore, and their application in World ID
quote: World ID uses an open-source protocol known as Semaphore that confirms verifications cannot be tracked to a person's identity, nor to verifications in other applications. World ID uses two circuits: Semaphore (to prove inclusion of a WorldId in the anonymity set) and the Semaphore Merkle Tree Batcher (SMTB) for efficient insert/delete on the underlying trees.

title: Anon Aadhaar — zero-knowledge protocol for proving Aadhaar identity
quote: Anon Aadhaar is a zero-knowledge protocol that allows Aadhaar ID owners to prove their identity in a privacy-preserving way — generate a ZK proof of identity by only revealing the information they want to share.

title: ZK Email — zero-knowledge proofs of DKIM signatures
quote: ZK Email proves a valid DKIM signature exists for an email without revealing the email's contents or the signature itself, enabling selective verification of email content (whether sender belongs to a specific organization, contains certain keywords) by leveraging the DKIM signature attached by the mail server. Stable 1.0 released November 2023; latest audited versions Fall 2024.

title: zkTLS — The Cornerstone of Verifiable Internet (zkPassport context)
quote: zkPassport combines international ePassport chip technology with ZKP to provide users with a secure and privacy-preserving identity verification method, supported by passports from over 100 countries based on global standards (ICAO Biometric Passport).

title: Clustering Deposit and Withdrawal Activity in Tornado Cash: A Cross-Chain Analysis
quote: 5.1–12.6% of withdrawals can already be traced to their originating deposits through address-reuse and transactional-linkage heuristics; adding the novel FIFO temporal-matching heuristic lifts the linkage rate by a further 15–22 percentage points. Heuristic 1 (address reuse) consistently produces the highest match rates on Ethereum, suggesting widespread address reuse.

title: Benchmarking ZK-Friendly Hash Functions and SNARK Proving Systems for EVM-compatible Blockchains
quote: Poseidon and Poseidon2 demonstrate superior memory usage and runtime during proof generation under Groth16. For SHA-256, Groth16 frameworks (rapidsnark, gnark, Arkworks) generate proofs faster than PLONK frameworks (Halo2, Plonky2). Groth16 produces the smallest proofs (~192 bytes) and the fastest verification (~3 ms) but requires a circuit-specific trusted setup; PLONK offers a universal trusted setup reusable across circuits.

title: Aztec: a Hybrid Public-Private ZK-Rollup
quote: Most zk-rollups advertise 'ZK' in their name but typically do not provide user privacy — the zero-knowledge property is used for succinctness rather than hiding data. While zkSync, StarkWare, and Scroll prioritize scalability, Aztec distinguishes itself by prioritizing privacy as its primary focus. Aztec separates public and private execution; private execution runs in a Private eXecution Environment (PXE) on the user's device.

title: The EU is about to outlaw and restrict some of the most prized features in crypto
quote: Crypto-asset accounts allowing anonymization of transactions, and accounts using anonymity-enhancing coins, are banned under EU AML Regulation 2024/1624 (adopted May 2024, in force July 1, 2027). The Council of the EU is looking to ban privacy coins like Monero and Zcash. The European Commission must report on whether to ban anonymous accounts and crypto mixers like Tornado Cash.

title: From Aztec to Zcash: The year 'pragmatic privacy' took root
quote: From Aztec to Zcash, 2025 was the year 'pragmatic privacy' took root — a deliberate move away from absolutist 'hide everything' designs toward selective-disclosure constructions (Privacy Pools' association-set proofs, Aztec's PXE, Railgun's per-asset shielded balances) that ship under uncertain regulatory regimes.

title: Privacy Pools Documentation (0xbow)
quote: Privacy Pools uses zero-knowledge proofs and an Association Set Provider (ASP) — a compliance layer that screens deposits and monitors transactions in real-time. Users cryptographically prove their funds aren't associated with illicit activity without revealing transaction details, a capability previous privacy tools lacked.

title: L2BEAT ZK Catalog — Worldcoin Semaphore
quote: World ID's anonymity-set construction documented and tracked by L2BEAT's ZK Catalog: Semaphore (group-membership ZK proof) plus Semaphore Merkle Tree Batcher (SMTB) used to insert/delete users from the underlying Merkle trees efficiently.

title: Announcing NoirJS: Privacy-Preserving ZK Applications In Your Browser
quote: NoirJS lets developers build privacy-preserving ZK applications that run client-side in the browser, with Barretenberg-class proving that previously required heavy backend hardware now feasible on consumer devices.

title: Aztec Documentation — Privacy-first zkRollup
quote: Aztec is privacy-first because its entire architecture is built around hiding data by default. The Aztec Network separates public and private execution, with private execution done in a Private eXecution Environment (PXE) running on the user's device. Private state is encrypted; only the user holding the keys can read it.

title: 0xbow Privacy Pools Core (open-source contracts)
quote: Privacy Pools by 0xbow is a compliant way to anonymously transact on Ethereum. 0xbow blocks illicit actors to ensure pool integrity.
